title: DO NOT MERGE: try to figure out the preserve-fds bug
body:
Add "ls -l /proc/self/fd" before running the test.
Might be useful to also run "lsof -p $$", what's the
right Go incantation for getting my own pid?

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>
```release-note
none
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [edsantiago] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Yay, [the flake triggered](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5116209468276736/html/int-podman-ubuntu-2204-rootless-host.log.html#t--podman-run-preserve-fds-invalid-fd--1)! Boo, there are no extra open fds. Unless `exec.Command()` closes fds??? That would be weird. I'll continue playing with this tomorrow.

Sigh. Failed, with both `ls` and `lsof` results... but I'm darned if I can figure out a useful difference between the [failed ubuntu log](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5795147463000064/html/int-podman-ubuntu-2204-rootless-host.log.html#t--podman-run-preserve-fds-invalid-fd--1) and the [passing one on fedora](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/4669247556157440/html/int-podman-fedora-36-rootless-host.log.html#t--podman-run-preserve-fds-invalid-fd--1).

@edsantiago Yes exec.Command closes all fds, see my comment on the issue:
> We use os/exec to start podman in the tests, it should not matter how many fds we leak in the test since os/exec will always unset all fds.
https://cs.opensource.google/go/go/+/refs/tags/go1.19.1:src/os/exec/exec.go;l=506-517
That makes me think the leak or bug must be somewhere in podman itself.

https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/15943#issuecomment-1260009822

It does not matter how many open fds the test has, in fact we cannot control this at all since they run in parallel.
So either the golang lib has a bug and does not close the fds in all cases or we have a bug in podman.

Ugh, you're right. I guess closing fds is user-friendly in some respects, it's just counterintuitive for someone who grew up on UNIX and C. Thanks for the reminder.

Do you have any suggestions on how else to track this down? Since this is consistently ubuntu-only, could it be something that ubuntu changed in the Go libraries, as a distro patch? Or a too-old version of Go?

Again, this is easy to fix in the test, I just don't want to hide a real bug.

I don't really know, all I see is that we have some custom c code which checks the fds:
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/84c85f4d9457fb35849fa93ba04814df681b9093/pkg/rootless/rootless_linux.c#L299-L338 
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/84c85f4d9457fb35849fa93ba04814df681b9093/pkg/rootless/rootless_linux.c#L274-L281
I notice there's no matching `closedir()` for the `opendir()` on line 300. Should there be?
Unable to reproduce the flake if I use `-focus` on the ginkgo tests.

@edsantiago: PR needs rebase.

<details>

Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.
@edsantiago is this still valid, needs a rebase.
This is an Ubuntu-only flake. We haven't run CI on Ubuntu in a month, so I'll just close this and reopen if we ever get Ubuntu back.
